title: Ericoides formosum
taxonomy: Ericoides formosum is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically in the family Ericaceae. It is a genus of flowering plants, and as such, it is also classified in the division Magnoliophyta, and the class Magnoliopsida. Within the class Magnoliopsida, it is further classified in the order Ericales, which comprises various species of flowering plants. The order Ericales is a part of the clade Asterids, which is a large group of flowering plants. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and evolutionary relationships.

intro: Ericoides formosum, also known as Formosa plant or Formosa willow, is a type of herbaceous perennial plant native to East Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ericoides formosum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is commonly prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in various herbal formulas to support respiratory health.
